page_meta.frost_note: FROST RISK — January is fully within the frost season. Protect all tender plants. Hard frosts possible. Mulch overwintering crops.
FROST RISK — January. Frost likely most nights. Protect containers; bring tender plants indoors. Forced bulbs safe indoors.
FROST RISK — February. Frosts still likely most nights. Soil mostly cold. Grow lights essential for indoor sowings.
FROST RISK — February. Nights still frosty. First spring bulbs braving the cold. Indoor seed starting begins in earnest.
FROST RISK — March. Frosts still common especially at night. Use cloches and row cover for all outdoor sowings.
FROST RISK — March. Nights still frosty but garden waking fast. Spring bulb peak. Do not move tender plants outdoors.
FROST RISK — April. Last frost ~April 15. Hold frost-tender transplants until after mid-month. Spring bulbs at peak.
FROST RISK — October. First frost ~Oct 15. Remove frost-killed annuals. Plant spring bulbs. Mulch tender plants before hard frost.
FROST RISK — November. Ongoing frost. Protected growing only for tender crops. Mild spells allow some outdoor work.
FROST RISK — November. Garden quietens. Winter-blooming shrubs begin. Leave perennial stems for wildlife and winter interest.
FROST RISK — December. Coldest month. Protect all growing crops. Check cloches and row covers regularly. Mild spells allow brief garden work.
FROST RISK — December. Midwinter. Winter-blooming shrubs at their glorious best. The garden rests and renews.
